title: GUIDELINES ON THE PROVISION OF FOOD INFORMATION FOR PRE-PACKAGED FOODS TO BE OFFERED VIA E-COMMERCE CXG 104-2024

sections.section: GUIDELINES ON THE PROVISION OF FOOD INFORMATION FOR PRE-PACKAGED FOODS TO BE OFFERED VIA E-COMMERCE CXG 104-2024
sections.summary: Document title and adoption note: Guidelines on provision of food information for pre‑packaged foods offered via e‑commerce (CXG 104‑2024), adopted in 2024.

Purpose: these guidelines aim to ensure consumers purchasing pre‑packaged foods via e‑commerce receive the information needed to make informed choices, comparable to information on the physical label.

Scope: applies to food information required or voluntarily provided on the product information e‑page for pre‑packaged foods offered via e‑commerce and certain aspects of its presentation, but does not apply to information that is required on the label at point of delivery under the General Standard for Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985).

Defines terms for e-commerce labelling of pre-packaged foods: 'at the point of delivery' (when consumers receive the food); 'e-commerce' (production, distribution, marketing, sale or delivery by electronic means as applicable to foods); 'food information' (information that is the subject of a Codex text about a pre-packaged food); 'prior to the point of e-commerce sale' (provided before consumers commit to ordering/purchasing); and 'product information e-page' (the consumer-facing transactional virtual space intended to facilitate informed e-commerce sale).

States that the general principles in Section 3 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods apply to food information shown on the product information e-page for pre-packaged foods offered for sale.

Mandatory label information required by the General Standard and other relevant Codex texts must be provided on the product information e-page prior to the point of e-commerce sale (except where those texts expressly provide otherwise, and excluding items required by GS sections 4.6 and 4.7.1), must include relevant nutrition labelling provisions, must carry a statement directing consumers to check the physical label before consumption, and is not subject to the small-unit exemption unless allowed by competent authorities, with all information provided free of charge to consumers.

sections.section: 6. OPTIONAL INFORMATION PRIOR TO THE POINT OF E-COMMERCE SALE
sections.summary: Section 7 of the General Standard applies to e-page information, and a statement may be provided on the product information e-page prior to sale to inform consumers of the relationship between best-before/use-by/expiration dates and the product shipping date or point of delivery.

Mandatory food information on product information e-pages must be clear, prominent and readily legible under normal use conditions, and presented in language(s) suitable to consumers in the country where the food is marketed and may be delivered.
